generated_comment:
A: Bhai, aadmi se bhi galti ho jati hai, par agar aurat ko pata ho ki woh shaadi-shuda hai phir bhi chipki rahe, toh ghar todne wali wahi hui na.
B: Haan, bilkul serial wali baat hai, aisi auratein hi aadmi ko phasa leti hain.
A: Bechare bachchon ka ghar barbaad kar deti hain, phir saara tamasha khada ho jata hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places primary blame on the woman in an affair while excusing the married man as merely making a mistake. It encodes homewrecker framing, seduction framing ('phasa leti hain'), and family protection rhetoric ('ghar todne wali').
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Jat man in Haryana. You are sitting with three male relatives at a family function after news broke in your district that a young woman from a nearby village was assaulted while walking home from a tuition class at 7pm. You are talking about what happened and what you think the girl's family should have done and what girls in villages need to understand about going out alone even in the evening.
